item.title: Snapchat announces ‘The Snappys,’ its first-ever creator awards show
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Snapchat is the latest social media platform to launch awards for creators, joining TikTok and Instagram.

item.title: An accountant won a big jackpot on Kalshi by betting against DOGE
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A tax accountant saw Elon Musk fans bidding up a Kalshi prediction market and saw a sure bet to make easy money.

item.title: Inside the story of the US defense contractor who leaked hacking tools to Russia
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The former boss of a U.S. hacking tools maker was jailed for selling highly sensitive software exploits to a Russian broker. This is how we first learned of his arrest, reported the story, and some of the unanswered questions we still have.

item.title: Samsung shows off new display tech that adds a privacy screen to apps and notifications
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new privacy tech uses different types of pixels to let you block certain apps and notifications from being viewed by others.

item.title: Wearable startup CUDIS launches a new health ring line with an AI-fueled ‘coach’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The wearable incentivizes healthy behavior with points that can be redeemed for health products.

item.title: Kalshi fined a MrBeast editor for insider trading on markets related to the YouTube star
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Kalshi fined the MrBeast editor, Artem Kaptur, for over $20,000.

item.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies—including bans on new construction.

item.title: Waymo to begin testing in Chicago and Charlotte
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Waymo this week will begin mapping and collecting data in Chicago and Charlotte. The move comes as Waymo announces it is currently operating its robotaxis fully autonomously in 10 US cities.

item.title: Gemini can now automate some multi-step tasks on Android
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Gemini on Android will be able to automate tasks involving rideshare requests, or grocery or food delivery, says Google.

item.title: OpenAI COO says ads will be ‘an iterative process’
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: COO Brad Lightcap noted that ads can add to the product experience of users if they are done right. He urged to give OpenAI a few months to see how the company fares in rolling out the product.

item.title: OpenClaw creator’s advice to AI builders is to be more playful and allow yourself time to improve
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Peter Steinberger talks about the creation of his viral AI agent OpenClaw and how being more "playful" makes for a better way to learn AI coding.

item.title: Have hard-won scaling lessons to share? Take the stage at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Apply to speak at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 by April 17 for a chance to lead a roundtable or breakout session for 1,000 founders and investors. If you’ve built, backed, or operated inside high-growth startups, your experience could shape how the next wave of founders scales.

item.title: About 12% of US teens turn to AI for emotional support or advice
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: General-purpose tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Grok are not designed for this use, making mental health professionals wary.

item.title: Americans now listen to podcasts more often than talk radio, study shows
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Recent findings also show that video podcasting -- while popular -- is not necessarily replacing audio shows.

item.title: 3 days left: Save up to $680 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Just 3 days left to save up to $680 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket. Offer ends on Friday, February 27 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't miss unparalleled, curated networking and valuable insights from 250+ tech leaders, and discover 300+ breakout innovations. Register now.

item.title: US tells diplomats to lobby against foreign data sovereignty laws
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered U.S. diplomats to lobby against countries' attempts to regulate how American tech companies handle foreigners' data.

item.title: Harbinger acquires autonomous driving company Phantom AI
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: It's the Los Angeles trucking startup's first acquisition — the latest in a series of moves to spin up new revenue streams.

item.title: Jira’s latest update allows AI agents and humans to work side by side
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Atlassian is unveiling "agents in Jira," which gives users the ability to assign and manage work given to AI agents the same as humans.

item.title: Y Combinator grad and AI insurance brokerage Harper raises $47M
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Harper is an AI-native insurance brokerage that just raised a $45 million combined Series A and seed, after being a member of YC's Winter 2025 cohort.

item.title: Amazon’s AI-powered Alexa+ gets new personality options
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Users will be able to choose from Alexa styles like Brief, Chill, or Sweet, Amazon says.
